note: Hand-maintained catalog of the best 10 free-tier-eligible providers for the Free-Tier Pool track. This is metadata only — no keys live here. `wire` selects which pool-adapters.cjs adapter handles the family; `baseUrl` is an editable prefill for the add-key form (same 'catalog fills it in, user can override' pattern as the existing Providers catalog tab); `tosRisk` surfaces each provider's own terms-of-service posture for personal/single-user proxy use (informational, not legal advice — see plugin/scripts/pool-router.cjs header). `typicalLimits` is a short human-readable summary. `models` is an EXHAUSTIVE researched (2026-07-05) per-model breakdown — every free-tier text-generation/chat-completion model each provider offers (audio/TTS, embeddings, image-gen, and moderation-only models excluded as unusable for a Claude Code chat backend), not a trimmed sample — context window in tokens, and whichever of RPM/RPD/TPM/TPD the provider actually publishes for its free tier (null, not guessed, for anything unconfirmed) — cross-checked between github.com/cheahjs/free-llm-api-resources and each provider's own official docs, official docs preferred on conflict. Shown in the add-key form (prefills Base URL, model datalist, and the advanced context/limit fields) and in the 'browse all models' reference table; never auto-applied without the user seeing it first. DeepSeek and OVH were dropped from the prior 12-provider list: DeepSeek's own API turned out to have no free tier at all (pay-per-token only, confirmed via official docs), and OVH's 2 RPM/no-context-data offering was the weakest of the set — Cloudflare Workers AI was promoted in to keep a genuine top 10. Cohere is deliberately absent: its terms explicitly forbid personal/household use.

providers.name: Z.ai (Zhipu) GLM
providers.typicalLimits: 2 fully free models (GLM-4.7-Flash/4.5-Flash) — RPM/RPD not published (account-gated)

providers.name: Groq
providers.typicalLimits: 12 free models — ~30 RPM (60 for Qwen3 32B), RPD 100–14,400, TPM 1.2K–70K

providers.name: Cerebras
providers.typicalLimits: 3 free models — 5 RPM · 30K TPM · 1M TPD, uniform across all

providers.name: GitHub Models
providers.tosNote: Free tier explicitly scoped to experimentation/prototyping. Also caps every request at 8,000 input / 4,000 output tokens regardless of the model's real context window — not a TPM/TPD limit, a per-request cap.
providers.typicalLimits: 35 free models (Copilot Free) — low tier 15 RPM/150 RPD, high tier 10 RPM/50 RPD, reasoning models 1–2 RPM/8–12 RPD

providers.name: OpenRouter (free models)
providers.typicalLimits: 19 free models — 20 RPM · 50 RPD shared account-wide (1,000 RPD after $10+ lifetime topup)

providers.name: SambaNova
providers.typicalLimits: 6 free models via $5/3-month trial credit — no fixed RPM/RPD published

providers.name: Cloudflare Workers AI
providers.tosNote: No anti-proxy clause found; covered by the general Self-Serve Subscription Agreement. Base URL includes your account id — fill in after Base URL. Limit is a shared 'neurons' compute budget, not a request or token count, so it doesn't map onto RPM/RPD/TPM/TPD — treat the numbers below as unavailable, not zero.
providers.typicalLimits: 42 free models sharing a 10,000 Neurons/day budget (resets 00:00 UTC) — no per-request RPM/RPD

providers.name: NVIDIA NIM
providers.tosNote: Trial ToS scopes free access to evaluation only, not production. Requires phone-number verification.
providers.typicalLimits: 48 free models — ~40 RPM shared across all, no published RPD/TPM/TPD

providers.name: Google Gemini
providers.tosNote: March 2026 ToS narrows free-tier scope to professional/business use; also geo-blocked in EU/UK/CH. Google's own docs no longer publish a static rate-limit table — these numbers come from the community aggregator only and may drift; check aistudio.google.com/rate-limit for your account's actual figures.
providers.typicalLimits: 7 free models — Flash family 5–15 RPM · 20–500 RPD · 250K TPM · up to 1M context

providers.name: Mistral AI
providers.typicalLimits: 35 free models via the "Experiment" plan (~1 req/sec, 500K TPM) plus Codestral (30 RPM · 2,000 RPD)
